segments.text: And the Lord said to Moses,
I will bring one more plague on Pharaoh and on Egypt. Afterward he will let you go from here. When he lets you go, he will surely drive you out of here altogether.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Speak now in the hearing of the people, and let every man ask from his neighbor and every woman from her neighbor, articles of silver and articles of gold.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And the Lord gave the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians. Moreover the man Moses was very great in the land of Egypt, in the sight of Pharaoh's servants and in the sight of the people.
Thus says the Lord: 'About midnight I will go out into the midst of Egypt;
segments.speaker: Moses (quoting The Lord)

segments.text: and all the firstborn in the land of Egypt shall die, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sits on his throne, even to the firstborn of the female servant who is behind the handmill, and all the firstborn of the animals.
segments.speaker: Moses (quoting The Lord)

segments.text: Then there shall be a great cry throughout all the land of Egypt, such as was not like it before, nor shall be like it again.
segments.speaker: Moses (quoting The Lord)

segments.text: But against none of the children of Israel shall a dog move its tongue, against man or beast, that you may know that the Lord does make a difference between the Egyptians and Israel.'
segments.speaker: Moses (quoting The Lord)

segments.text: And all these your servants shall come down to me and bow down to me, saying, 'Get out, and all the people who follow you!' After that I will go out.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Then he went out from Pharaoh in great anger.
But the Lord said to Moses,
Pharaoh will not heed you, so that My wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: So Moses and Aaron did all these wonders before Pharaoh; and the Lord hardened Pharaoh's heart, and he did not let the children of Israel go out of his land.
